PAHouse.com
McNeill celebrates House passage of digital advertising tax legislation
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
June 11, 2026 | 2:04 PM
HARRISBURG, June 11 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill said she was pleased to vote in favor of the Digital Ads Tax Bill (H.B. 1678), which passed the House Tuesday and would update Pennsylvania’s gross receipts tax to include the sale of digital advertisements.
“Currently, mega-corporations such as Google, Meta, Amazon and Microsoft make huge profits by using our personal data to direct nuisance ads at us,” said McNeill, D-Lehigh. “These pop-up ads, banner ads, and even video ads that begin playing immediately, make using the internet worse. It’s time these companies start to pay for doing this.”
By implementing a digital advertising tax, Maryland raised $170 million from Big Tech companies to fund public education in just a few years. Pennsylvania can do the same and gain revenue to prevent property tax hikes for seniors, McNeill said.
The bill is now with state Senate for further consideration.

McNeill celebrates passage of resolution recognizing child safety advocates
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
June 10, 2026 | 10:43 AM
HARRISBURG, June 10 –
On Monday, the Pennsylvania House of Representatives adopted a resolution that honors Pennsylvania Court Appointed Special Advocates volunteers for their advocacy and efforts in promoting the safety and wellbeing of Pennsylvania’s children, according to the resolution’s prime sponsor, state Rep. Jeanne McNeill.
“CASA is a network of 21 local programs in 32 counties in Pennsylvania,” said McNeill. “These programs, comprised of more than 1,000 volunteers, advocate for more than 2,000 children who have been subjected to abuse as well as neglect. These volunteers work to ensure that children’s needs are met while in the dependency system, as well as assisting in obtaining safe and permanent housing.”
McNeill, who is chair of the House Children and Youth Committee, said it was a resolution that she was proud to introduce and was happy to see adopted by an overwhelming margin. McNeill also credited Judge Doug Reichley, president judge of the Lehigh County Court of Common Pleas, for providing vital information about the key role Court Appointed Special Advocates perform.
“I am thankful to my colleagues that voted in support of these children to give them the better future they deserve,” said McNeill. “While this is a small thank you to these volunteers, it is an important one as we acknowledge the significance of their work.”

McNeill announces two awards totaling $107K in state funding for community improvement projects
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
May 21, 2026
HARRISBURG, May 21 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill, today announced that two projects in her community are recipients of state funding totaling $107,181 through the Local Share Account grants program.
“As a state legislator, I appreciate the opportunity to help the communities I represent by finding funding for worthy projects,” said McNeill. “Securing these funds will help improve the quality of life enjoyed by the people in these communities. The West Broad Street renovations will improve the area for bicyclists and pedestrians. The lighting improvements at our parks in Whitehall Township will allow for greater usability and safety.”
The grants awarded include:
$80,000 to Whitehall Township for lighting improvements at parks throughout the township – The project focuses on upgrading lighting systems across several parks within the Township Recreation System to improve safety, efficiency, and usability. West Catasauqua Playground, Stiles Playground, Cementon Park, and Hokendauqua Park will all receive new LED lighting, with existing poles, wiring, and conduit reused wherever feasible. At each site, outdated lighting infrastructure will be removed, new conduit and wiring added as needed, and new brackets, adapters, or pole-mounted fixtures will be installed. Across the various courts – including tennis, basketball, and volleyball – dozens of new LED fixtures will be positioned to provide consistent, modern illumination. Altogether, these improvements will modernize the parks’ recreation facilities while reducing long-term maintenance and energy consumption.
$27,181 to Bethlehem City for renovations to West Broad Street – The proposed project includes redesigning intersections with curb extensions and realigned crosswalks, upgrading pedestrian lighting and bus stops, adding bicycle lanes, and modernizing traffic signals to improve safety and efficiency. The project will improve pedestrian and bicyclists' safety.
The grants were approved on Wednesday by the Commonwealth Financing Authority. The Local Share Account Programs were created through the 2004 PA Racehorse Development and Gaming Act, which provides for the distribution of gaming revenue to support projects in the public interest within the commonwealth.

McNeill’s office to hold monthly veterans assistance May 18
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
May 15, 2026 | 12:37 PM
WHITEHALL, May 15 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill is alerting local veterans that her next monthly support program will be held Monday, May 18 at her district office in Whitehall.
A representative from the American Legion will be available for appointments at McNeill’s district office, 1080 Schadt Ave. Appointments are available between 10 a.m. and 2 p.m.
The service officer will be available to assist veterans and their families with benefits and services available to them. The representative will also be available to discuss pensions and other compensation, as well as education, healthcare and death benefits.
Legion membership is not required; however, anyone interested in speaking with the adviser should schedule appointments by contacting McNeill’s office at (610) 266-1273.

PAHouse.com
Lehigh County legislative delegation statement on potentially voter-disenfranchising error with vote-by-mail ballots
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
May 5, 2026 | 3:42 PM
State Reps. Mike Schlossberg, Peter Schweyer, Jeanne McNeill and Ana Tiburcio and state Sens. Lisa Boscola and Nick Miller offered the following statement on the error by Lehigh County leadership in sending incorrect vote-by-mail ballots for the May 19 primary election.
“At a time when the integrity of our elections is under constant attack from extremists who want to end vote-by-mail the last thing we need to do is hand them more rhetorical ammunition courtesy of this careless error by our county leadership.
“This mistake must be fixed immediately. Voters who applied to vote-by-mail in the May primary election must get their correct ballots as quickly as possible, and it is our sincere hope that the county will take every possible action to do just that.
“Furthermore, county leadership must take responsibility and explain how this happened and how they will make sure this never happens again. We need a solid accounting to make sure every voter’s ballot counts and we are ready to explore every legal remedy to assure the residents of Lehigh County that their vote is protected.
“This error could be disenfranchising to the people of Lehigh County. We all deserve better.”
Pennsylvania's vote-by-mail law was passed in 2019. Sen. Boscola was the prime sponsor of the enabling legislation in the state Senate.

They demanded that correct ballots be sent to affected voters as quickly as possible and that county officials explain how the mistake occurred and how it will be prevented in the future. McNeill office to hold annual Senior and Veterans Expo event April 10
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
April 8, 2026
WHITEHALL, April 8 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill is informing the community that her annual Senior and Veterans Expo will take place at 9 a.m. this Friday, April 10 at the St. Luke’s University Health Network Sports Center in Whitehall.
Local organizations will be in attendance offering help and resources to seniors and veterans alike at the center located at 3321 7
th
St., Whitehall, PA 18052.
“There are so many resources available to residents living in the commonwealth,” said McNeill. “While that is a great thing, it can also make it overwhelming and difficult for Pennsylvanians to know exactly what is for them and what they qualify for. That is why events like these are so important, because they streamline that process and it is why I hold this event every year. The more those in my community learn and get connected with these resources, the higher their quality of life is and that is my number one priority as a state legislator and servant to my community.”
Anyone with questions about the event should contact McNeill’s office at (610) 266-1273.

McNeill’s office to hold monthly veterans assistance Feb. 23
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
February 20, 2026 | 3:25 PM
WHITEHALL, Feb. 20 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill is alerting local veterans that her next monthly support program will be held on Monday, Feb. 23 at her district office in Whitehall.
A representative from the American Legion will be available for appointments at McNeill’s district office, 1080 Schadt Ave. Appointments are available from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m.
The service officer will be available to assist veterans and their families with benefits and services available to them. The representative will also be available to discuss pensions and other compensation, as well as education, health care and death benefits.
Legion membership is not required; however, anyone interested in speaking with the adviser should schedule appointments by contacting McNeill’s office at (610) 266-1273.

McNeill appointed to Lehigh-Northampton Airport Authority
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
February 12, 2026 | 12:25 PM
HARRISBURG, Feb. 12 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill announced today that she has been appointed to the Lehigh Northampton Airport Authority.
“I’m honored to be appointed to the Lehigh Northampton Airport Authority and grateful for the trust placed in me,” said McNeill. “Our airport is a vital economic engine for the Lehigh Valley, supporting jobs, commerce, and regional connectivity. I look forward to collaborating with my fellow board members to ensure the airport operates safely and efficiently, while fostering long-term growth and community benefits for the 133rd Legislative District.”
McNeill was appointed to the authority by Lehigh County Executive Josh Siegel.
The mission of the Lehigh-Northampton Airport Authority is to operate its facilities safely and efficiently, pursue innovative and competitive growth, and help make the region the community of choice for businesses and residents.

McNeill expands mental health advocacy by joining Clubhouse of Lehigh County Advisory Board
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
January 22, 2026 | 3:53 PM
HARRISBURG, Jan. 22 –
Continuing her advocacy and presence in the community, state Rep. Jeanne McNeill announced today that she has joined the Clubhouse of Lehigh County Advisory Board.
“I have long been a strong advocate for mental health services, community-based supports, and policies that strengthen opportunities for individuals and families across our region and our commonwealth,” said McNeill. “I am always honored to be granted an opportunity like this one that allows me to engage more with my community and continue to understand the issues that matter to them the most and that they want me to work through with my colleagues in Harrisburg.”
During her time with the Carbon Lehigh Intermediate Unit 21, McNeill worked closely with members and staff of the clubhouse.
The Clubhouse of Lehigh County’s mission is to offer persons with mental illness a safe and healing environment wherein each person is given the opportunity to explore their personal and vocational potentials to their fullest and to receive support in achieving their goals.

McNeill announces more than $1 million in new CFA grants
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
January 20, 2026 | 1:41 PM
HARRISBURG, Jan. 20 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill announced today that $1.48 million in state funding has been awarded to two projects in her Lehigh County legislative district.
“This funding is about investing in the people and places that make our district strong,” said McNeill, D-Lehigh. “By bringing state resources back home, we’re helping to improve quality of life, strengthen our local economy, and address the needs our residents have shared with me. I’m grateful to everyone who helped make this possible.”
The Commonwealth Financing Agency approved funding for the following projects in McNeill’s district:
$1,310,000 to construct a parking garage connected to the Lehigh Valley International Airport located in Hanover Township.
$170,681 for Front Street and Liberty Street drainage improvements in Catasauqua Borough.
Funding was administered by the CFA, which was established in 2004 as an independent agency to administer Pennsylvania’s economic stimulus packages.

PAHouse.com
McNeill applauds over $2 million in funding for her community
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
December 18, 2025 | 1:31 PM
HARRISBURG, Dec. 18 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill announced today that more than $2 million in new grant funding from the statewide Local Share Account, and Greenways, Trails and Recreation Program is being awarded to multiple organizations for projects across Lehigh County.
In McNeill’s district, grants include:
$1 million to purchase an aircraft rescue and firefighting vehicle for use at the Lehigh Valley International Airport.
$500,000 to purchase a paving machine to be used in Whitehall Township.
$350,000 to construct a retaining wall in Catasauqua Borough.
$250,000 to purchase a new pumper fire truck for use in Catasauqua Borough.
$181,900 for Coplay Borough for a Pickleball Court project.
$50,459 to purchase firefighting equipment to be used in Fountain Hill Borough.
$39,572 to purchase two speed trailers for use in Catasauqua Borough.
$20,230 for the Jefferson Street Recreation Center Study.
“I am proud I was able to help secure these grants for the communities I represent,” said McNeill. “This funding will help improve our parks and trails, create more opportunities for outdoor recreation, and support the local organizations working hard to make Lehigh County a better place for families and residents. I am always thankful to my colleague in the Senate, Sen. Nick Miller, for his help in acquiring these grants.”

PAHouse.com
Bill combatting illegal foreign nicotine e-cigarettes targeting youthsent to governor’s desk
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
December 17, 2025 | 4:31 PM
HARRISBURG, Dec. 17 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill’s legislation combatting the growth of illegal, foreign-manufactured e-cigarettes containing nicotine, including flavored products designed to appeal to youth, is set to become law.
House Bill 1425 was voted on concurrence with Senate amendments on Tuesday.
The legislation would establish a statewide
electronic nicotine delivery systems Directory that would list all legal vapor products authorized for sale in Pennsylvania. Modeled after the commonwealth’s highly effective Tobacco Product Directory, the ENDS Directory would be administered and enforced by the Office of Attorney General.
“This legislation gives Pennsylvania the tools it needs to protect kids, support law-abiding retailers, and crack down on illegal vapor products that have flooded the market,” said McNeill. “Right now, there is no clear way for states to determine which products are legally authorized by the FDA. This bill closes that gap and brings accountability to a system that has been exploited for too long. It was gratifying that my colleagues in the House and Senate, and our attorney general, Dave Sunday, focused on children’s health and made this a bipartisan effort.”
Manufacturers will be required to annually certify that their products have received FDA marketing authorization or that their Premarket Tobacco Applications remain under review. Enforcement will be handled by the Department of Revenue, the Office of Attorney General, and state and local law enforcement.
Pennsylvania previously led efforts to protect youth by raising the minimum age to purchase tobacco and vapor products from 18 to 21 in 2019—action later adopted nationwide.
This bill now heads to the governor’s desk to be signed into law.

PennDOT: Green Light-Go Program
Who May Apply:
Municipalities and planning organizations.
Use:
To improve existing traffic control signals, such as LED replacement, traffic signal retiming, and studies, among other projects.
Funds:
Up to $40 million is available and projects require a 20% match.
Application Deadline:
Pre-application scoping forms will be accepted until January 2, 2026. PennDOT will provide comments by February 28, 2026. The full application window will be between March 1, 2026, and March 31, 2026.

PennDOT website

Who May Apply:
Volunteer fire companies in rural areas or communities under 10,000 in population
Use:
To prevent, control, and suppress the threat of wildfires through the purchase of wildfire suppression equipment, wildfire protective gear, mobile radios, installation of dry hydrants, wildfire training, or wildfire prevention and mitigation

Funding is provided by the U.S. Department of Agriculture. The maximum grant for 2025 is $15,000.

To develop competitive sites for businesses to relocate or expand within Pennsylvania.

$400 million in funding is available.

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis and approved on a quarterly basis

Technical and trade schools, school districts, post-secondary academic institutions, workforce investment boards, economic development organizations, and non-profit organizations. For-profit entities are not eligible.

Funds are available on an annual basis and vary based on recommendations and constraints set in the annual budget.

Applications will be accepted and evaluated on an ongoing basis as long as funds remain available.

Pennsylvania residents whose total household income is below 400 percent of the Federal Income Poverty Level.

Applications will be accepted and evaluated on a first come, first served basis as long as funds remain available. Please note applications must be received within six months of the date of the vehicle purchase.

PAHouse.com
McNeill applauds passage of legislation to strengthen the child care workforce
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
June 25, 2025 | 4:08 PM
HARRISBURG, June 25 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill is heralding the final passage of legislation she wrote to support the childcare workforce for childcare providers participating in the Child Care Works program.
“Too many parents in Pennsylvania are forced to reduce their hours or leave the workforce altogether because they can’t find affordable, reliable, and quality childcare,” said McNeill. “There is a persistent shortage of childcare workers, driven in large part by low wages that make it difficult for providers to recruit and retain staff. I am proud of my colleagues to have voted in support of my legislation that would help solve this.”
House Bill 506 would utilize funding proposed by the governor in the 2025-26 budget to support the childcare workforce through recruitment and retention bonuses for licensed childcare providers participation in the Child Care Works program. On average, those bonuses would result in an additional $1,000 per teacher annually, providing a meaningful resource to help childcare providers raise wages and stabilize the workforce.
“This investment would not only help stabilize our childcare workforce and provide quality care and education to our youngest learners, but it will also support the broader workforce and strengthen Pennsylvania’s economy by keeping parents from leaving their jobs due to high costs or lack of childcare,” said McNeill.
House Bill 506 now goes to the state Senate for consideration.

PAHouse.com
McNeill applauds legislation keeping PA youth safe from illicit vapor products
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
June 23, 2025 | 4:45 PM
HARRISBURG, June 23 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill applauded today’s passage of her electronic nicotine delivery system directory legislation by the House of Representatives.
This legislation would create a directory, similar to Pennsylvania’s widely effective Tobacco Product Directory, for vapor products.
“With our current system, Pennsylvania struggles to enforce legal marketing law of these vapor products, meaning that many retailers who in good faith have decided against selling vapor products that can’t be legally marketed in the United States are losing significant market share and revenues to those who do sell them,” McNeill said. “The directory created by this legislation would solve this by allowing the enforcement of penalties to retailers and wholesalers who sell unlisted nicotine products.”
If signed into law, enforcement of this law would be handled by the Department of Revenue, the Office of Attorney General, and state and local law enforcement.
Pennsylvania has been a leader in combatting youth access to vapor products by enacting legislation in 2019 to raise the minimum age to purchase vapor and tobacco products from 18 to 21.
H.B. 1425 now heads to the full Senate for consideration.

PAHouse.com
McNeill releases statement in support of House passage of H.B. 350
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
June 23, 2025 | 4:25 PM
HARRISBURG, June 23 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill offered the following statement regarding today’s House passage of H.B. 350, a bill on which she is a co-prime sponsor:
“It is critical that we have clear guidance on how to establish legal child-parent relationships to help facilitate family stability and judicial consistency. Unfortunately, this guidance is missing in our current statutory law, resulting in the creation of a roadblock that could stand in the way of Pennsylvania’s children and families.
“Passing House Bill 350 in the House means one step closer to this becoming enshrined in law. One step closer to a framework of clear parameters in support of the parent and child relationship.
“As the chair of the House Children and Youth Committee, the level and quality of support that children in Pennsylvania and their families receive is very important to me. Oftentimes, these can be the deciding factor of whether a child is successful or not. I was proud to stand with my colleagues today to vote in support of Pennsylvania families.”

PAHouse.com
House OKs McNeill bill prohibiting early termination fees of leased vehicles for medical reasons
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
June 23, 2025 | 3:39 PM
HARRISBURG, June 23 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill announced today that her bill, H.B. 205, that would prohibit early termination fees on leased vehicles for medical reasons, has passed the PA House.
“Being diagnosed as medically unfit to drive is a life-altering experience,” said McNeill. “It is unfair for someone to bear the financial burden of paying off a lease and termination fees for a vehicle they can no longer drive legally, especially when their diagnosis will often lead to a loss of income due to them dealing with their health issues.”
If signed into law, H.B. 205 would only require a person returning the leased vehicle to be responsible for any damage incurred. It would also prohibit financing companies and dealerships from charging early termination fees or penalties, and people could not be held accountable for the remaining balance on the lease.
If a medical professional determines that a person is unfit to drive safely, the person must report that determination to PennDOT as required by law.
House Bill 205 now goes to the state Senate for consideration.

PAHouse.com
McNeill: $1.5 million to the Lehigh Valley International Airport
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
June 4, 2025 | 9:27 AM
HARRISBURG, June 4 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill announced today that state funds totaling $1.5 million has been awarded to the Lehigh Valley International Airport in the 133
rd
Legislative District.
The Lehigh Valley International Airport will use the $1.5 million in state funding to construct a parking structure to enhance terminal commercial development.
“In many instances, a large portion of an airport’s total revenue is related to commercial development,” McNeill said. “This funding will not only lead to an increase in dollars generated for the airport, but it will also create jobs during the deck’s construction and from the resultant businesses it will support. This is great news for Lehigh Valley International Airport, the people in my district, and to the entire Lehigh Valley. We are fortunate to have an amazing airport like LVIA in our community, and this state investment recognizes that.”
This project will be funded by the state Capital Budget through the Aviation Transportation Assistance Program, which is supported by bonds.
Pennsylvania’s robust aviation industry employs over 226,000 people, provides nearly $12.5 billion in payroll and employment benefits, and has a $34 billion annual economic impact.

McNeill, Miller help acquire funding for community improvement projects
Rep. Jeanne McNeill
July 16, 2026 | 3:42 PM
HARRISBURG, July 16 –
State Rep. Jeanne McNeill and Sen. Nick Miller today announced $4.2 million in grant funding from the Redevelopment Assistance Capital Program, which will support multiple construction projects within their community.
“These projects reflect the kind of investments that make a real difference in our communities,” said McNeill. “Whether it is transforming a long-vacant property into a productive new development, improving infrastructure at our regional airport, or creating modern recreational space for local families and student-athletes, this funding will have a lasting impact. I am glad to see these projects moving forward because they will strengthen our local economy, improve quality of life, and build on the assets that make our region a great place to live and work.”
“These strategic investments will strengthen our neighborhoods and position the Lehigh Valley to lead,” said Miller. “Every one of these projects represents conversations with local leaders and continuous advocacy to ensure these projects receive the investments they deserve. I am proud to have secured this funding alongside Rep. McNeill as we continue fighting for brighter futures for every Lehigh Valley resident.”
The following projects in the district have been awarded funding:
$2.75 million
to the Lehigh-Northampton Airport Authority for improvements to the Lehigh Valley International Airport Parking Garage. The garage will consist of five levels, providing approximately 1,000 additional public parking spaces, and more than double the quantity of ADA-accessible parking spaces. This project also includes procurement of the following major equipment: up to four large capacity elevators; parking access and revenue control equipment; 14 electric vehicle charging stations; various life/health/safety equipment.
$1.2 million
to Whitehall Township to redevelop a long-abandoned site which will be repurposed for commercial or mixed-use development.
$250,000
to the Allentown Central Catholic High School Inc. to construct a multi-purpose athletic facility, including a turf field, running track, a small press box, and spectator seating. It also entails the construction of restroom facilities, snack stand, a garden area, storage facilities for equipment, a lighting and sound system, and parking.
RACP is a commonwealth grant program administered by the Office of the Budget for the design, acquisition, and construction of regional economic, cultural, civic, recreational, and historical improvement projects.
These projects are authorized in the Redevelopment Assistance section of a Capital Budget Itemization Act, have a regional or multi-jurisdictional impact, and generate substantial increases or maintain current levels of employment, tax revenue, or other measures of economic activity.

ai_summary.summary: State Rep. Jeanne McNeill and Sen. Nick Miller announced $4.2 million in state grant funding awarded to three projects in the Lehigh Valley: $2.75 million for a parking garage expansion at Lehigh Valley International Airport, $1.2 million for redevelopment of an abandoned site in Whitehall Township, and $250,000 for a new athletic facility at Allentown Central Catholic High School. The funding comes from Pennsylvania's Redevelopment Assistance Capital Program, a standing state grant program administered by the Office of the Budget. The legislators announced the grants but did not themselves vote on or directly authorize this specific funding.
